pr.title: Start splitting out character-specific items, match spacies' laser and phantasm/illusion items
pr.author: r-burns

summary: PR 937 began separating character-specific item code out of the large item assembly unit and matched the spacies' laser and illusion/phantasm item logic in C. It added src/melee/it/items/itfoxlaser.c and itfoxillusion.c plus headers, moved many remaining character item assembly blocks into asm/melee/it/items/*, wired the new objects into obj_files.mk, and added item-variable structs for FoxLaserVars and FoxIllusionVars. The change also tightened related declarations, including making Item_80268B18 return the spawned HSD_GObj and changing ftFx_SpecialS_GetCmdVar2 from u32 to s32.
what_changed: Removed roughly 19k lines from the monolithic asm/melee/it/it_27CF.s and reintroduced many character-item assembly ranges as separate asm/melee/it/items/*.s objects for Mario fireball, Kirby cutter beam, Link items, and Ness PK items.

Added matched C implementations for spacies' laser item behavior in src/melee/it/items/itfoxlaser.c, including ItemStateTable it_803F67D0, spawn helpers it_8029C504/it_8029C6A4/it_8029C6CC, angle normalization, velocity/rotation/scale handling, collision reflection/bounce callbacks, and destroy callback it_8029CCF8.

Added matched C implementations for Fox illusion/Falco phantasm item behavior in src/melee/it/items/itfoxillusion.c, including ItemStateTable it_803F6818, ghost JObj creation/removal, owner-linked ghost position updates via ftFx_SpecialS helpers, lifetime transitions, and destroy callback it_8029D948.
Created item-specific headers itfoxlaser.h and itfoxillusion.h and moved related declarations out of the broad it_27CF.h surface.
Created src/melee/it/itCharItems.h with FoxLaserVars and FoxIllusionVars, then included it from src/melee/it/types.h and added foxlaser/foxillusion members to Item.xDD4_itemVar.
Changed Item.xC58/xC5C/xC60 from separate scalar fields into Vec3 xC58, which is used by the laser mirror path through lbVector_Mirror.
Changed Item_80268B18 from void to HSD_GObj* so newly matched spawn helpers can directly receive the spawned item object after Item_8026862C.

Updated src/melee/it/it_803F3100.c to include the new item headers, remove duplicate local prototypes/externs, give the item logic table an explicit size of 118, and annotate many entries with character-item comments such as Fox laser, Falco laser, Fox illusion, Falco phantasm, Link bomb, and Ness PK fire.
Updated ftFox special-N and special-S source files to include the new item headers, and changed ftFx_SpecialS_GetCmdVar2's return type from u32 to s32 in both source and header.
Updated obj_files.mk to insert the newly split asm objects and the two new matched C objects in the item object list.
smart_moves: Used a hybrid split: only the targeted spacies items were matched in C, while other character-item ranges were moved to separate asm files. This reduced monolithic asm debt without requiring every adjacent character item to be matched at once.

Kept address-style function and state-table names for the newly matched code, which preserved compatibility with existing item logic table references while still isolating code in item-specific files.

Introduced FoxLaserVars and FoxIllusionVars before adding them to Item.xDD4_itemVar, making item-local variable offsets explicit and reusable instead of scattering raw xDD4/xDD8/xDDC field interpretations.
Made Item_80268B18 return the spawned GObj rather than forcing matched spawn helpers to duplicate lower-level spawn logic or rely on side effects.
Moved laser and illusion prototypes into item-specific headers and included those headers from fighter code, reducing dependence on the catch-all it_27CF.h declarations.

For matching, retained small helper inlines such as normalizeAngle and local fabsf, plus explicit unused stack slots where needed, rather than over-abstracting the decompiled code away from the original codegen.
decomp_lessons: When a large asm file contains many independent character item articles, splitting unmatched neighbors into per-item asm files can create room to match one article family without destabilizing the entire translation unit.

Article/item state tables are natural anchors for splitting item code: matching it_803F67D0 and it_803F6818 made the Fox/Falco laser and illusion/phantasm logic table entries easier to type and include.
If decompiled spawn code needs the created object, check whether a wrapper such as Item_80268B18 should return an existing lower-level result instead of inventing alternate control flow.
Typed item variable structs inside Item.xDD4_itemVar are useful for matching article-specific code, especially where offsets otherwise look like unrelated raw fields.

Fighter/item boundaries should be represented with specific headers when possible; spacies' item code depended on ftFx_SpecialS ghost/cmd-var helpers and the fighter code depended on laser/illusion spawn declarations.
Changing a field grouping to Vec3 can unlock natural calls such as lbVector_Mirror, but downstream initialization and all offset users should be audited carefully.
naming_conventions: Character-item source files follow lower-case item names under src/melee/it/items/, e.g. itfoxlaser.c and itfoxillusion.c, with matching headers itfoxlaser.h and itfoxillusion.h.
Character-item variable structs use descriptive PascalCase names with Vars suffix: FoxLaserVars and FoxIllusionVars.
Matched symbols retained address-derived names such as it_8029C504, it_8029D094, it_803F67D0, and it_803F6818 where semantic names were not yet established.
The new shared character-item variable header is named itCharItems.h and is included from it/types.h alongside other item variable headers.
Item logic table comments use semantic article names in braces, such as Fox laser, Falco laser, Fox illusion, Falco phantasm, Link hookshot, Young Link arrow, and Ness PK thunder.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved object order in obj_files.mk by inserting the new per-item asm objects and matched C objects before the remaining asm/melee/it/it_803F5468.s.o and asm/melee/it/it_27CF.s.o entries.
Reduced asm/melee/it/it_27CF.s by moving large address ranges into named item asm files before matching only the spacies ranges in C.
Used ItemStateTable arrays as exact data structures for item callbacks, keeping state IDs and callback order explicit.
Used inline angle normalization around 0..2*M_PI and manual f32 math constants/calls such as cosf, sinf, atan2f, and M_PI-derived rotations for Fox laser matching.
Used explicit local unused arrays in several functions to preserve stack layout/codegen in matched C.
Modeled laser reflection by storing a previous position Vec3 and calling lbVector_Mirror on item velocity and item->xC58.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review bodies were present in the provided PR slice, so no human reviewer feedback could be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type FoxLaserVars and FoxIllusionVars fields.
Find later fixes around Item.xC58 initialization after it became a Vec3.
Find subsequent PRs that match Link bomb, Link boomerang, Link hookshot, Link arrow, Ness PK fire, PK flush, or PK thunderball after this asm split.
Find later reviews or commits touching Item_80268B18's return type and callers.
Find PRs that replace address-derived names in itfoxlaser.c or itfoxillusion.c with semantic names.
